headline: Target posts lower 2025 earnings as Q4 sales dip
summary: In the quarter ended 31 January 2026, net sales declined 1.5% to $30.45bn from $30.91bn in the prior-year period.

content.title: Target posts lower 2025 earnings as Q4 sales dip
content.summary: In the quarter ended 31 January 2026, net sales declined 1.5% to $30.45bn from $30.91bn in the prior-year period.

headline: Iran war pressures consumer costs: What does it mean for retailers?
summary: Target (TGT) narrowly beat earnings expectations but posted another quarter of declining sales, leaving investors focused on the company's upcoming analyst day and long-term turnaround plans. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma joins Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi to explain how geopolitical tensions, higher gas prices, and other macro uncertainties are impacting consumer behavior and what that means for Target, Best Buy (BBY), and other retailers. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid

content.title: Iran war pressures consumer costs: What does it mean for retailers?
content.description:
Target (TGT) narrowly beat earnings expectations but posted another quarter of declining sales, leaving investors focused on the company's upcoming analyst day and long-term turnaround plans.

Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma joins Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi to explain how geopolitical tensions, higher gas prices, and other macro uncertainties are impacting consumer behavior and what that means for Target, Best Buy (BBY), and other retailers.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid

headline: Target Stock Continues To Disappoint

headline: Target's new CEO says fresh eyes and respecting core values will help him regain customer trust
summary: Target plans to spend billions of dollars this year to end a persistent sales slump and to regain its image as a fun place to shop for stylish clothing, housewares, beauty products and packaged food at affordable prices.  The CEO guiding the turnaround plan is Michael Fiddelke, who stepped into the chief executive role last month but has worked for Target for more than 20 years.  Fiddelke, who previously served as chief operating officer, has his work cut out for him.

Fiddelke, who previously served as chief operating officer, has his work cut out for him.
